the father sets a reading challenge where the number of pages read each day follows a quadratic sequence. the sequence is defined as p(n) = n² + 3n + 1. the first day, the children read 5 pages, the second day 12 pages, and the third day 21 pages, which matches the formula. the general formula for the number of pages read on the n-th day is p(n) = n² + 3n + 1. the total number of pages read over 30 days is calculated by summing the sequence from n = 1 to n = 30. the sum of the first 30 terms of the sequence is 30(30 + 1)(2*30 + 1)/6 = 4965. the children will read a total of 4965 pages by the end of the 30 days. the pen is mightier than sword. the pen is mightier than sword.
